body.p1: HobbyHash Coin is the coin name. HOBC is the ticker. The project focus is practical SHA256 mining for people who want to learn, run nodes, mine from home, and understand the chain without fake market claims or profit promises.
body.p2: HOBC documentation is written for home miners, hobby miners, solo miners, small ASIC setups, node operators, and users who want to keep control of their own wallet backups.
hero.lead: Learn the basics of HOBC wallets, nodes, mining, and explorer tools.
hero.title: Getting Started
list.avoid.1: Do not treat mining as guaranteed income.
list.avoid.2: Do not download wallets or nodes from random mirrors.
list.avoid.3: Do not expose RPC ports to the public internet.
list.avoid.4: Do not share wallet files, private keys, seed phrases, or passwords with anyone claiming to be support.
list.avoid.5: Do not use exchange, BTC, BCH, or other non-HOBC addresses as HOBC mining payout addresses.
list.can_do.1: Download the official Windows standalone wallet or Linux node software when real files are available on the downloads page.
list.can_do.2: Run a full node and help relay blocks and transactions.
list.can_do.3: Mine HOBC with compatible SHA256 mining hardware.
list.can_do.4: Use the explorer when available to inspect blocks, transactions, and addresses.
list.can_do.5: Keep wallet backups offline and test that you understand your recovery path before holding larger balances.
meta.description: Getting started with HobbyHash Coin nodes, wallets, and mining.
meta.title: Getting Started
step.1: Read the Wallet Guide before receiving funds.
step.2: Read the Security Guide before downloading or installing wallet software.
step.3: If you want to run infrastructure, read the Linux Node Guide.
step.4: If you want to mine, read the Mining Guide and Pool Stats Guide.
step.5: Use support if you are unsure which pool, wallet, or node setup applies to you.
